title: OAuth2 Token Refresh Loop Causing Authentication Service Overload
symptoms: OAuth2 token refresh endpoint receiving 3.2 million requests per minute
Authentication service CPU at 100% across all instances
Token refresh requests from mobile app with 0ms interval between retries
All login attempts timing out for web and API users
Mobile app client logs showing rapid token refresh retry loop
root_cause: Mobile app OAuth client bug incorrectly parsing valid token responses as errors, triggering immediate retry loops across all 35,000 connected mobile users
resolution: Deployed server-side rate limiting on token refresh endpoint per client ID, pushed emergency mobile app update with fix, and scaled auth service to handle the transitional load
preventive_actions: Implement per-client rate limiting on all OAuth endpoints
Add exponential backoff requirement in OAuth client SDK
Create server-side circuit breaker for token refresh storms
Implement OAuth client behavior monitoring with anomaly detection
Add mandatory code review for OAuth client library changes

timeline.event: Token refresh rate spikes from mobile app clients
Auth service CPU reaches 100% across all instances
P1 alert fires, Aisha Khan begins investigation
Mobile app client identified as source of refresh storm
Server-side rate limiting deployed on refresh endpoint
Auth service load reducing, web and API logins recovering
Emergency mobile app update submitted for review

embedding_summary: OAuth2 token refresh loop from mobile app bug overwhelmed authentication service with 3.2 million requests per minute. 47,000 users across all platforms unable to authenticate for 25 minutes. Resolved by server-side rate limiting and emergency mobile app update.
